name: Green bean / snap bean (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw pod, Phaseolus vulgaris)

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 169961 (SR Legacy, Beans, snap, green, raw)
components.evidence: Moisture 90.3 g/100g; the matrix is dilute, ~10% dry matter.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Glucose (dextrose) is the co-dominant free sugar; a primary fermentable carbon source.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Fructose co-dominant with glucose.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor disaccharide.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Not detected (0 g). Current record listed 'Maltose'; effectively absent in raw pod.

components.name: galactose
components.evidence: Not detected.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Low storage starch in the immature pod; a slow-release glucose polymer.

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Sum of free sugars; dominant carbon source is glucose+fructose (~2.9 g/100g together).

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Structural polysaccharides (cellulose/pectin); largely non-fermentable by most model organisms.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Amador et al. 1997 Eur Food Res Technol doi:10.1007/s002170050091; De la Torre 1994 J AOAC 77:1056 (PMID 8069112); MDPI Agronomy 2021 doi:10.3390/agronomy11040803
components.evidence: Malic acid is the DOMINANT organic acid of green-bean pods by HPLC (multiple studies). Was MISSING from the FooDB profile. A verified fresh-weight mg/100g value could not be extracted from open sources; see 'total titratable acidity' for a quantitative anchor (~0.16 g/100g FW as citric-equivalent, of which malate is the largest share).

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Amador et al. 1997 doi:10.1007/s002170050091; De la Torre 1994 PMID 8069112
components.evidence: Second most abundant organic acid after malic; confirmed present by HPLC. Fresh-weight amount not retrievable from open sources (paywalled tables).

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: oxalatecontent.com citing Scielo Cienc Tecnol Aliment; Harvard oxalate list gives 21.8 mg
components.evidence: Green beans are a low-oxalate vegetable; reported 21.8-65 mg/100g (65 used here, note wide range). Both organic acid and mild antinutrient (Ca chelation).

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Trace organic acid detected by HPLC in green-bean pods; present at low level, not quantified in open source.

components.name: total titratable acidity (as citric equivalent)
components.source: MDPI Agronomy 2021 doi:10.3390/agronomy11040803 (1.6 g citric/100g DRY weight) x ~10% dry matter (USDA moisture 90.3%)
components.evidence: Total organic-acid pool of the fresh pod, malate-dominant. Quantitative anchor for the malic+citric+fumaric fraction.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Total crude protein; source of the amino-acid pool below.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.source: USDA SR (Beans, snap, green) amino-acid profile
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (protein-derived; free Asn/Asp also high in pods).

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Second most abundant; major N and carbon source.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Limiting sulfur amino acid.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.evidence: P source for nucleotides/phospholipids.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: Enzyme cofactor.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates the redundant BiGG iron species (Fe2+, Fe3+) into total Fe.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Ultratrace.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Low but present.

components.name: betaine (glycine betaine)
components.evidence: Trace; genuine osmolyte.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Gamma-tocopherol also present (trace).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin A; RAE ~35 ug.

components.name: lutein + zeaxanthin
components.evidence: Dominant carotenoid; genuine, no BiGG met.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Very low-fat matrix.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid despite low total fat.

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: Animal metabolite (muscle phosphagen); not synthesized by plants. FooDB predicted/implausible in a plant pod.

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: Sulfonic amino acid essentially absent from higher plants; animal-associated. FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
remove.reason: Oxidation artifact of methionine, not a defining nutrient; fold into L-methionine.

remove.name: Racemethionine
remove.reason: DL-methionine is a synthetic racemate; the natural pod contains only L-methionine (already listed). Artifactual.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Redundant BiGG compartment/oxidation-state duplicates of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' (total Fe = 1.03 mg/100g).

remove.name: CO2 CO2 / O2 O2 / H+ / H2O H2O (as nutrients)
remove.reason: Gases/solvent/proton are medium/atmosphere terms, not food-derived growth nutrients (water retained explicitly as solvent 90.3 g).

missing_added: malic acid (DOMINANT organic acid, was absent)
total titratable acidity anchor (0.16 g/100g as citric-eq)
individual sugars quantified: glucose 1.51, fructose 1.39, sucrose 0.36 g/100g
quantified vitamins (C, B1-B9, E, K1) and minerals (K, P, Ca, Mg, Na, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se)
keep_uncovered: p-coumaric acid, trans-sinapic acid, ferulate (ferulic acid)
L-DOPA, dopamine, serotonin, histamine (minor legume amines - genuine but low)
levoglutamide (L-glutamine), citrulline, hydroxyproline, aminoadipic acid, ADMA (minor free amino compounds)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources: free glucose (1.51) + fructose (1.39) g/100g, backed by low starch (0.88) and modest sucrose. Organic-acid pool is malate-dominant (was entirely missing from the FooDB record); malic and citric fresh-weight amounts could not be extracted from open (paywalled) sources so are reported present-but-unquantified with a titratable-acidity anchor (~0.16 g/100g as citric-eq). Amino-acid profile from USDA SR (cooked profile used as representative; raw values near-identical) is Asp/Glu-dominant. Matrix is high-water (90.3%), very low-fat. Record was relatively clean of heavy-metal noise; main removals were animal metabolites (creatine, taurine) and redundant BiGG iron species.
